draft_picks.match: Orlando Magic vs Los Angeles Lakers
draft_picks.reasoning: Lakers (45-25, L10 9-1) are a hot team with strong credentials on the road. Key factor: Orlando's franchise player Franz Wagner is confirmed OUT (injury management). Without Wagner, Orlando's offense is significantly diminished. Orlando NetRtg +0.9 vs Lakers +1.4 — essentially equal on season stats, but Orlando without their best player tilts the edge to LA. Austin Reaves is Questionable (hip soreness) which caps confidence — applied -5 for GTD status. L15 unavailable for both teams — applied -5 where decisive. Road game for Lakers factored in. Confidence: 65 (base for Lakers quality/opponent missing franchise player) -5 (Reaves GTD) -5 (L15 missing) = 55. EV = (0.55 × 2.28) - 1 = 0.254 (25.4%). Strong edge. Lakers odds at 2.28 are in target range (1.70-2.50). Stake at 15% tier adjusted -10% for Reaves GTD.
anchor_players: Franz Wagner (OUT — Orlando franchise player, injury management)

draft_picks.match: New Orleans Pelicans vs Cleveland Cavaliers
draft_picks.reasoning: Cleveland (43-27, L10 6-4) is a legitimately strong team priced as road underdog at 2.45 against New Orleans (25-46, L10 6-4). NetRtg gap: Cleveland +4.2 vs New Orleans -3.8 = 8.0 point gap — a significant signal. Cleveland's Donovan Mitchell is healthy (not on injury report). Jarrett Allen is OUT (knee) which is a meaningful loss at center but not the franchise player. New Orleans Pelicans are near tank-tier (25-46) with a volatile L10 6-4 that does not override their season trajectory. Pelicans have no major injury news that would boost them. Cleveland is a seeding-motivated team fighting for top-6 East position. Road game and Allen absence each apply -5 confidence. L15 missing but with 8.0 NetRtg gap, it is less decisive — applying reduced -3 penalty. Final: 65 (base) -5 (road) -5 (Allen OUT) -2 (L15 partial) = 53. EV = (0.53 × 2.45) - 1 = 0.299 (29.9%). Strong edge at speculative tier.

draft_picks.match: San Antonio Spurs vs Indiana Pacers
draft_picks.reasoning: San Antonio Spurs (52-18, L10 9-1, NetRtg +7.2) host a severely depleted Indiana Pacers squad. Indiana's franchise player Tyrese Haliburton is confirmed OUT (ACL tear). Additionally, multiple Pacers starters are Questionable: Nembhard (calf contusion), McConnell (soreness), Nesmith (management), Jackson (calf). Indiana's season NetRtg is -8.9 — one of the worst in the league. NetRtg gap: Spurs +7.2 vs Pacers -8.9 = 16.1 points — an enormous gap that strongly supports the spread. Spurs DefRtg 111.5 is elite; Pacers OffRtg 110.1 is poor. Without Haliburton running the offense, Indiana's attack collapses further. Stephon Castle Questionable (hip tightness) is a minor concern (-3 confidence). Large spread caution (-5 for historical cover rate on 19+ point spreads) applied. Base 68 (elite team vs gutted opponent, massive NetRtg gap) -3 (Castle GTD) -3 (large spread caution) = 62. Spread odds 1.90 in target range (1.80-2.30). EV = (0.62 × 1.90) - 1 = 0.178 (17.8%). Strong edge.
anchor_players: Tyrese Haliburton (OUT — Indiana franchise player, ACL tear)
Stephon Castle (Questionable — San Antonio, hip tightness)
Victor Wembanyama (active — Spurs franchise player, not on injury list)

settled_bets.match: Miami Heat vs Los Angeles Lakers
settled_bets.reasoning: Scout identified Miami Heat ML at 2.25 based on superior NetRtg (+3.1 vs Lakers +1.4), home court advantage, and Lakers' hot streak flagged as potentially luck-driven. At commit time, odds improved to 2.38 (+0.13 in our favour), consistent with news that Luka Doncic (Questionable, Right Hip) and LeBron James (Questionable, Left Foot Arthritis) are both uncertain for Lakers — two franchise-level players potentially unavailable. Austin Reaves also Questionable. Heat are missing Jaquez Jr. (confirmed OUT, was Questionable at Scout), Andrew Wiggins (OUT, new), and Terry Rozier (OUT, new), but their core rotation (Butler, Adebayo) remains intact. Net injury impact heavily favours Heat: Lakers potentially missing two franchise players outweighs Heat's role-player absences. Books have moved from 2.25 to 2.38, partially pricing in LAL risks, but 2.38 still offers strong value. EV at commit odds: (0.53 × 2.38) - 1 = 0.2614 = 26.1%, well above 0.05 threshold. Odds within ML valid range (1.65–2.50). Confidence maintained at 53 (Speculative): LAL injury upside offset by Heat's additional absences. Stake: 10% of €1000 = €100. Original stake unchanged per rules (do not increase even on improved odds). Total exposure: 10% of bankroll.
anchor_players: Luka Doncic (Questionable — Lakers, franchise player, Right Hip Soreness)
LeBron James (Questionable — Lakers, franchise player, Left Foot Arthritis)
Jaime Jaquez Jr. (OUT — Heat, Left Hip Tightness, upgraded from Questionable)
Andrew Wiggins (OUT — Heat, Sesamoiditis, new at commit)
Terry Rozier (OUT — Heat, Not With Team, new at commit)

settled_bets.reasoning: Celtics NetRtg +7.9 vs Grizzlies -2.9 = 10.8 pt gap (well above 6.0 threshold). Memphis confirmed tanking team (24-44, L10: 2-8) with catastrophic roster depletion confirmed at commit time: Aldama OUT, Caldwell-Pope OUT, Clarke OUT, Edey OUT, Gibson DOUBTFUL. GG Jackson no longer listed as Doubtful (potentially active), which explains the 1.5-point spread compression from -15.5 to -14.0. Boston (46-23, L10: 7-3) anchor players Tatum, Brown, Holiday all confirmed active — not on injury report. Spread moved from -15.5 to -14.0 (easier to cover, effectively line movement in our favor). Odds moved marginally from 1.91 to 1.90 (-0.01, within 0.05 drift allowance). EV = (0.80 × 1.90) - 1 = 0.52. Confidence unchanged at 80 — Memphis depletion confirmed, Celtics core intact, strong motivation for seeding. Strong edge confirmed at commit time.

cancelled.match: Brooklyn Nets vs New York Knicks
cancelled.reason: Spread line moved from -17.5 to -11.5 (6-point shift against the pick) — movement exceeds 0.30 threshold with no clear explanation available at commit time. Per rules: cancel if line moved > 0.30 for any reason without explicit justification.

cancelled.match: Minnesota Timberwolves vs Portland Trail Blazers
cancelled.reason: Odds moved against from 2.15 to 1.98 (-0.17). Per movement-against rules, confidence reduced by 10 (55 → 45). Confidence below 50 floor — do not confirm. EV recalculated at adjusted confidence: (0.45 × 1.98) - 1 = -0.109, well below the 0.05 requirement. Additionally, Portland now implied as slight favorite in spread market (-1.5), indicating market has fundamentally repriced this matchup since Scout.

commit_report_data.report:
### CONFIRMED — Boston Celtics -14.0
- Match: Memphis Grizzlies vs Boston Celtics | Tip: 2026-03-21T00:00:00Z
- Pick: Boston Celtics -14.0 | Scout odds: 1.91 (spread -15.5) | Commit odds: 1.90 (spread -14.0) | Move: -0.01 odds, spread shifted 1.5pts
- ML: 7.0/1.11 | ATS: -14.0 (1.92/1.90) | O/U: N/A
- Stake: €225 | Pot: €427.50 | Conf: 80/100 | EV: 0.52
- Decision: All three anchor players (Tatum, Brown, Holiday) confirmed active per NBA official injury report. Memphis depletion confirmed — Aldama OUT, Caldwell-Pope OUT, Clarke OUT, Edey OUT, Gibson DOUBTFUL. Spread compressed 1.5 points (likely GG Jackson no longer Doubtful, now active), which actually makes the bet easier to cover. Odds moved only -0.01 within drift allowance. EV of 0.52 remains excellent. Strong edge fully intact at commit time.

---

### CANCELLED — New York Knicks -17.5
- Reason: Spread line moved massively from -17.5 to -11.5 — a 6-point shift against the pick with no available explanation. This far exceeds the 0.30 movement threshold requiring cancellation. The -17.5 line no longer exists in the market.
- Odds at cancel: 1.87 (was 1.92 at scout time); spread -11.5 (was -17.5)

---

### CANCELLED — Minnesota Timberwolves ML
- Reason: ML odds moved against from 2.15 to 1.98 (-0.17). Per movement-against rules, confidence reduced by 10 points (55 → 45). Confidence of 45 falls below the 50 minimum floor for any confirmed bet. EV recalculated at adjusted confidence: (0.45 × 1.98) - 1 = -0.109, well below the 0.05 floor. Portland is now priced as a slight spread favorite (-1.5), confirming the market fundamentally repriced this game since Scout.
- Odds at cancel: 1.98 (was 2.15 at scout time)

---

### LATE SCOUT SUMMARY
No new picks added. All games within the valid time window (Clippers @ Dallas 00:30, 76ers @ Utah 01:30, Bucks @ Suns 02:00) either had no valid odds in the feed or all available ML odds fell outside the 1.65–2.50 valid range. The two Scout anomalies (Pistons/Warriors, Nuggets/Raptors) remain unexplained — Detroit's injury report was absent from the feed and Denver's roster situation is unclear, preventing action on either. The Quantity gate (max 1 new pick) was not reached as no pick met all criteria simultaneously.

**Slate summary:** A cautious commit session — only 1 of 3 draft picks confirmed, with the Celtics -14.0 spread representing a genuinely strong edge (EV 0.52, confidence 80) against a depleted Memphis roster. The Knicks spread was cancelled due to an unexplained 6-point line shift, and the Timberwolves ML was cancelled after odds movement dropped adjusted confidence below the actionable threshold. Total committed stake of €225 (25% of bankroll) is well within the 70% exposure cap, preserving €675 for future sessions.
